transcript: With spring and summer around the corner it's a great time to take your strength training routine outside. Here are a few exercises that you can try in almost any park. Squats are a great lower body exercise and they incorporate most of the major muscle groups in your legs and your core. Step ups are a great way to incorporate balance training into your lower body strength training. Planks are a great upper body exercise but they can be pretty difficult. By adding an incline it decreases the intensity and makes it so everybody can be successful. Push-ups can also be a very challenging exercise but by adding an incline I promise they'll be easier. For push-ups, step-ups and squats aim for 10 to 15 reps for 1 to 3 sets and for planks try to hold for 5 to 15 seconds and then progressively add more time you never know how long you'll be able to go for let us know on facebook how your outdoor strength training routine is going
